articles.abstract: The storied British automaker is going all-electric, and this fall it will unveil its new flagship, with deliveries coming next year. The strategy is distinctive but risky and has drawn some prominent detractors.

articles.webHeadline: Mass Mournings, 6 Days and 2 Countries: Iran Prepares to Bury Supreme Leader
articles.printHeadline: Iran Plans Huge Belated Funeral for Its Slain Supreme Leader
articles.abstract: Long-delayed funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed during U.S.-Israeli strikes at the war’s outset, are set to begin Friday. For the regime, it is a critical moment to demonstrate that it has endured.

articles.webHeadline: U.S. Hopes for Investment in Iran Would Break With Decades of History
articles.printHeadline: U.S. Hopes For Iran To Integrate Globally
articles.abstract: Senior U.S. officials have said Iran would be richly rewarded for changing its stance on the United States. Iran’s leaders have rejected such a bargain in the past.

articles.webHeadline: Drone Flights Over NATO Sites Show Europe’s Vulnerability, Experts Say
articles.printHeadline: Drones Seen Over NATO Raise Alarms About Russia
articles.abstract: A new study, backed up by analysts and political leaders, says frequent drone incursions near military assets mark a Russian campaign to probe defenses and gather information.

articles.webHeadline: Russia Bombards Ukrainian Capital With Deadly Wave of Attacks
articles.printHeadline: Moscow Rains Revenge on Kyiv, Killing Dozens
articles.abstract: At least 27 people were killed in drone and missile strikes on Kyiv that damaged several residential buildings across the city, the authorities said.

articles.webHeadline: Chanel Is Buying the World’s Oldest Shirt Maker
articles.printHeadline: Good Fit Sought
articles.abstract: The French luxury group is acquiring Charvet, a Parisian brand that dates to the 1830s, in a bid to add some men’s wear savoir-faire to its portfolio.

articles.webHeadline: The World Has Failed the Uyghurs
articles.printHeadline: China Is Erasing My Culture
articles.abstract: If the global community can’t stop China’s atrocities against the Uyghurs, it must help us keep our culture alive in exile.

articles.printHeadline: U.S. Eases Punitive Measure It Imposed on Iraq in April to Thwart Iran’s Influence
articles.abstract: The Trump administration had halted the deliveries as part of its efforts to pressure the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.

articles.printHeadline: Arena Is Symbol of a Global Trend
articles.abstract: High growth rates and surging exports in Vietnam mask deep frustrations in one of Asia’s most ambitious countries.

articles.webHeadline: Can You Embrace A.I. Without Layoffs? This Company Says It’s Trying.
articles.printHeadline: An Attempt to Embrace A.I. Without Layoffs
articles.abstract: The German software giant SAP says it is betting that employees can reinvent jobs instead of eliminating them. Experts are divided on whether it will work.

articles.printHeadline: Arrests Double To 2,000 a Day In Push by ICE
articles.abstract: The agency has doubled its daily arrest numbers without the fanfare of last year’s large urban operations, sowing fear in immigrant communities.
